prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear, a CPU is kind of like the brain of a computer. It’s the little chip inside that thinks about everything and tells the computer what to do—like when you’re playing a game or writing a story. It doesn’t understand words or pictures like we do, but it follows very simple instructions really, really fast. I don’t know all the tiny details, but I remember it’s what makes the whole thing come to life when you turn it on. Just like how my brain helps me remember your favorite cookies, the CPU helps the computer remember and do things!
